prevalence.source: Brazilian neonatal/perinatal surveillance syntheses / Ministério da Saúde
prevalence.trend: slowly decreasing with improved perinatal care; higher rates persist in resource-limited settings

burden.dalyPerCapita: high per-case; concentrated in neonatal mortality and neurodevelopmental sequelae
burden.mortalityRate: Case-fatality commonly ~5-20% depending on severity and facility
burden.source: WHO / IHME GBD neonatal sepsis estimates

riskFactors: poor hand hygiene and overcrowding in neonatal units
vulnerablePopulations: settings with limited infection prevention resources
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical signs (temperature instability, respiratory distress, lethargy) with lab support (blood culture, CRP/procalcitonin) per neonatal sepsis protocols

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no screening; early recognition protocols in maternity and NICU settings
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Improving with timely antibiotics and NICU support; outcomes vary by facility capacity

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Neonatal units follow sepsis bundles; culture availability varies; clinical diagnosis often drives early treatment
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Empiric antibiotics (ampicillin + gentamicin for early-onset; broader coverage for late-onset based on local resistance) plus supportive NICU care
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Regional disparities in NICU capacity, antimicrobial resistance in some centers, and delays in referral from remote areas
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Expanding neonatal care networks and standardized sepsis protocols in many maternity hospitals
